water.name: Echo Canyon Reservoir
aliases: Echo Canyon Reservoir State Recreation Area

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — CR 25-16: Fishing Seasons and Regulations, January 1, 2026 – December 31, 2027
sources.quotedText: Echo Canyon Reservoir: Open year around. Daily: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Possession: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 20 may be black bass. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Echo Canyon Reservoir — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Echo Canyon Reservoir was constructed in 1969 in the Meadow Valley Wash, approximately 10 miles downstream from Eagle Valley Reservoir in Lincoln County... Rainbow trout, Brown trout, White crappie, Largemouth bass, Black bullhead... 70 surface acres with a maximum depth of 27 feet.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: Echo Canyon Reservoir is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit is 5 trout plus 15 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Possession limit is 10 trout plus 30 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 20 may be black bass.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish (no more than 10 black bass). Possession limit: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish (no more than 20 black bass).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. Possession: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which not more than 20 may be black bass.

parameters.note: No hour-of-day restriction; fishing permitted any hour of the day or night.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
